readiness.label: needs_attention
readiness.title: Needs attention before launch
readiness.summary: 8 issue(s) need review before this page should be considered launch-ready.

topIssues.title: Title is very short
topIssues.explanation: A very short title may not explain the page clearly.
topIssues.whyItMatters: This signal helps ShipReady decide whether the page is easy for crawlers, previews, and visitors to understand.

developerDetails.rawDescription: A very short title may not explain the page clearly.

topIssues.title: Search engines do not have a clear preferred URL.
topIssues.explanation: This page is missing a canonical URL, which helps search engines understand the main address for this content.
topIssues.whyItMatters: Without it, duplicate or alternate URLs can be harder to consolidate.

developerDetails.rawDescription: The raw HTML does not include a canonical link.
developerDetails.fixStrategy: Add a canonical link in the page head or framework metadata config.
developerDetails.recommendation: Add a canonical link that points to the preferred public URL for this page.

topIssues.title: No favicon links found
topIssues.explanation: Favicons and app icons help the site look complete in tabs, bookmarks, and device surfaces.
topIssues.whyItMatters: This signal helps ShipReady decide whether the page is easy for crawlers, previews, and visitors to understand.

developerDetails.rawDescription: Favicons and app icons help the site look complete in tabs, bookmarks, and device surfaces.

topIssues.title: Missing og:url
topIssues.explanation: Open Graph metadata should include the canonical page URL.
topIssues.whyItMatters: This signal helps ShipReady decide whether the page is easy for crawlers, previews, and visitors to understand.

developerDetails.rawDescription: Open Graph metadata should include the canonical page URL.

topIssues.title: Missing og:type
topIssues.explanation: Open Graph metadata should include a type such as website.
topIssues.whyItMatters: This signal helps ShipReady decide whether the page is easy for crawlers, previews, and visitors to understand.

developerDetails.rawDescription: Open Graph metadata should include a type such as website.

topIssues.title: Your site does not describe itself in structured data yet.
topIssues.explanation: Structured data helps search and AI tools understand what your site represents.
topIssues.whyItMatters: This is useful for clarity, but it usually needs factual review before ShipReady should write it.

developerDetails.rawDescription: No structured data script tags were found in the raw HTML.
developerDetails.recommendation: Add conservative, factual JSON-LD such as WebSite or Organization when appropriate.

topIssues.title: Your site does not have crawl instructions yet.
topIssues.explanation: A robots file gives crawlers basic instructions and can point them to your sitemap.
topIssues.whyItMatters: For supported projects, ShipReady can safely create this file without changing existing code.

developerDetails.rawDescription: No robots.txt file was found at the origin root.

topIssues.title: Your site does not have a sitemap yet.
topIssues.explanation: A sitemap lists the pages you want crawlers to discover.
topIssues.whyItMatters: For supported projects, ShipReady can safely create a simple sitemap file.

developerDetails.rawDescription: No sitemap.xml file was found at the origin root.

planAction.title: Add JSON-LD with review
